mission.title: keda: Add support for the "Value" metric type in addition to "AverageValue"
mission.description: Add support for the "Value" metric type in addition to "AverageValue". Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current keda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation.

steps.title: Review keda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
### Proposal

Except for the CPU and memory scalers, all scalers, including the external scaler, only support the `averageValue` metric type. The proposal is to allow for other metric types (specifically `value`).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for the "Value" metric type in addition to‚Ä¶
steps.description:
Add support for `ValueMetricType` in all scalers except cpu/memory by introducing a new optional `metricType` field to the ScaleTrigger spec. The field will default to `AverageValueMetricType` to maintain backward compatibility.

Notes:
- The `Utilization` metric type is not supported for external metrics, only `Value` or `AverageValue`.
- The CPU/memory scalers will continue to support the `Utilization` and `AverageValue` metric types.
- The fallback capability will continue to be supported

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for the "Value" metric type in addition to‚Ä¶" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Add support for `ValueMetricType` in all scalers except cpu/memory by introducing a new optional `metricType` field to the ScaleTrigger spec. The field will default to `AverageValueMetricType` to maintain backward compatibility.

Notes:
- The `Utilization` metric type is not supported for external metrics, only `Value` or `AverageValue`.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
